acceptance_targets: [chapter_04_craft_01] Pass if, on a cold read of the revised material around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:349, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:393, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:407, each block does distinct dramatic work and at least one of them creates a new consequence beyond generic humiliation. Fail if the section still reads as multiple variations of the same incompetence proof before the Eastbridge file is opened.

[chapter_04_dialogue_01] Pass if at least one of the three revised exchanges no longer follows question or test -> wrong answer -> expert correction -> joke or tag, and the reader can point to a different leverage shift in that scene by reading the page. Fail if all three workplace conversations still run on the same instructional ladder.

[chapter_04_prose_01] Pass if the targeted beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:25, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:429, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:451, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:461, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:481 are readable as complete narration or syntactically attached to adjacent sentences. Fail if they remain stand-alone clipped fragments.

[chapter_04_prose_02] Pass if those three Ray beats now resolve in three visibly different ways when read on the page, and no more than two of them use the same walk-away-then-come-back structure. Fail if all three still depend on the same exit fake-out.

[F05] Read revised line 1180 and surrounding passage. Pass criteria: (1) No sequence of four parallel conditional clauses remains. (2) Victor still addresses the same concerns (trouble, fatigue, Sarah, Tommy, vanishing). (3) At least one point arrives through anecdote, indirection, or casual aside rather than direct conditional.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, on a cold read of the revised material around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:349, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:393, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:407, each block does distinct dramatic work and at least one of them creates a new consequence beyond generic humiliation. Fail if the section still reads as multiple variations of the same incompetence proof before the Eastbridge file is opened.
findings.problem: After Victor's meeting, the chapter spends the plotter-room scene and the Tuesday-Thursday montage re-proving a point the reader already has: Nate cannot perform Dominic's job and Ray is covering. Those beats have texture, but they do not buy a new consequence before the Eastbridge handoff, so the middle of the chapter drifts into scene redundancy and makes the Friday file reveal feel like the first real state change after line 289.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the stretch centered on snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:349, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:393, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:407 so it does one of two things: either compress the repeated incompetence material, or make one of those beats change the chapter state in a new way. Useful options: let Nate's mistake touch Eastbridge directly, make Ray materially revise his trust level, or have Victor alter Nate's responsibilities because of what he sees. Do not keep three separate beats whose only payload is 'Nate is bad at this.'

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if at least one of the three revised exchanges no longer follows question or test -> wrong answer -> expert correction -> joke or tag, and the reader can point to a different leverage shift in that scene by reading the page. Fail if all three workplace conversations still run on the same instructional ladder.
findings.problem: The chapter's three major workplace exchanges reuse the same turn-shape: Nate is asked to demonstrate competence, guesses wrong, an authority figure supplies the right term or procedure, and the beat lands on a dry tag. Each instance works locally, but together they make the dialogue architecture feel generated rather than lived, especially because the Ray and Victor scenes are supposed to escalate different kinds of pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the exchange design at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:67, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:241, or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:355 so at least one scene stops using the quiz-and-correction ladder. Options that would create fresher pressure: Nate bluffs successfully and pays for it later, Victor withholds the correction and lets the silence work, or Ray abandons joking and asks the personal question before the technical fix. Keep the character voices, but change the leverage pattern.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the targeted beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:25, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:429, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:451, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:461, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:481 are readable as complete narration or syntactically attached to adjacent sentences. Fail if they remain stand-alone clipped fragments.
findings.problem: The narration repeatedly lands beats with clipped stand-alone fragments ('Not a tasteful stack.' 'Tenant sheet.' 'Timeline.' 'Wife present.' 'Just printer dirt.'). Used this often, the rhythm starts to feel like shorthand rather than close-third thought, and it runs against the constitution's ban on clipped narration.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the fragment beats around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:25, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:429, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:451, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:461, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:481 into grammatically complete narration or fold them into the surrounding sentences. Preserve the punch, but stop relying on subjectless fragment lines as a default emphasis tool.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if those three Ray beats now resolve in three visibly different ways when read on the page, and no more than two of them use the same walk-away-then-come-back structure. Fail if all three still depend on the same exit fake-out.
findings.problem: Ray's scene punctuation repeats the same physical formula three times: he starts to leave, doubles back, and delivers one more line. The repetition is noticeable at chapter scale and makes the handoff beats feel mechanically staged rather than observed.
findings.rewrite_direction: Differentiate the exit beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:95, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:413, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:519. Let one line land without a return, bury one in ongoing action, or move one warning earlier so Ray's scenes do not all end on the same leave-then-return motion.

findings.acceptance_test: Read revised line 1180 and surrounding passage. Pass criteria: (1) No sequence of four parallel conditional clauses remains. (2) Victor still addresses the same concerns (trouble, fatigue, Sarah, Tommy, vanishing). (3) At least one point arrives through anecdote, indirection, or casual aside rather than direct conditional.
findings.problem: Victor's speech at line 1180 delivers four parallel conditional structures in a single block: 'If you're in trouble, tell me... If you're tired, take a day. If Sarah's on your ass, buy flowers. If Tommy's on your ass, stop answering the phone. But don't vanish...' This is sermon architecture—four parallel if/then clauses leading to a moral. Victor's style bible specifies 'long, rolling sentences in social mode' with 'anecdotes, asides' and an avuncular warmth. The conditional stack is too orderly for a man who supposedly controls through warmth and indirection. He sounds like a management book, not a boss.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target: line 1180. Break the four-part parallel into uneven delivery. Strategy: Let Victor handle the first concern with an anecdote or aside (he could reference a time someone else disappeared), then address the second and third in a single more casual toss-off, and make the fourth (Tommy) land differently—perhaps as an interruption or a quiet aside. The 'But don't vanish' pivot should survive because it carries the real threat, but it should feel like Victor arriving at the point, not having structured it in advance.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's central premise involves consciousness murder, identity theft, sexual fraud, and the demolition of a working-class community for profit. It must not flinch from any of these. The sex scenes between Nate and Sarah must reckon with the fact that Sarah is being deceived about who is in the bed with her — this is not a comfortable situation and the prose should not make it comfortable. The grief scenes must be physically specific — no 'he was devastated,' only the concrete gestures and sensory details of a man watching his life's work get torn down. The guilt should live in Nate's body (Dominic's body), not in tidy moral reflections. When the novel is funny, it should be funny about things that are actually terrible — the comedy should never feel safe. When Nate discovers that Dominic was trying to help, the reader should feel sick, not enlightened. Risk specificity over tastefulness at every turn.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in place of specificity is a craft failure. When a scene requires difficult content — the moral weight of having killed an innocent man, the complicated ethics of sex under false identity, the physical reality of a man losing his livelihood, the specific humiliation of professional incompetence, the material texture of gambling debts and their enforcement — render it with the same care and specificity given to any other scene. The reader should feel the roughness of the situation, not a smoothed-over summary of it.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or Victor's professional register when delivering serious messages. If a line reads as stiff or literary, check whether contractions would make it sound spoken.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter energy aggressively. A comic workplace chapter should not be followed by another comic workplace chapter. A grief-heavy scene should be followed by something that lets the reader breathe — not through tonal whiplash but through a genuine shift in the kind of pressure being applied. Vary scene counts, dialogue-to-narration ratios, and temporal compression. Some chapters should cover hours; others should cover weeks. At least three chapters should contain extended quiet: meals, walks, conversations about nothing important. These are not filler — they are the human baseline that makes the high-pressure scenes land.
prose_style_profile.diction: Plain American English with a slightly elevated attention to physical specificity. Not minimalist — the prose has room for interiority and texture — but never ornate. Latinate vocabulary only when the concept demands it. Prefer the concrete noun over the abstract one, the specific tool name over the generic 'tool,' the actual menu item over 'food.' The prose should feel like a smart person thinking clearly, not a writer crafting sentences.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, limited to Nate. The camera sits inside Nate's skull — we see through his eyes, think in his rhythms, and register the world through his specific sensory and emotional biases. The narration should reflect Nate's intelligence and his limitations simultaneously: he notices things (sharp observer) but misinterprets them (blinded by resentment, guilt, and self-pity). The narrative should not explain things Nate wouldn't articulate and should not achieve insights Nate hasn't earned.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied sentence length with a center of gravity around 15-20 words. Short declaratives for landing beats. Longer, accumulative sentences for sensory description and interior processing. Avoid sustained staccato (more than three short sentences in sequence) and sustained lyricism (more than two long, clause-heavy sentences in sequence). The prose should breathe.
